new_relationships.evidence: Biofortification is a core strategy within nutrition-sensitive agriculture, directly enhancing nutritional content of crops through breeding to address hidden hunger and micronutrient deficiencies
Drought tolerance breeding is a primary mechanism for crop-level climate adaptation, bridging Crop Improvement and Climate Adaptation domains
CIMMYT's Global Wheat Program uses advanced molecular breeding tools including genomic selection as part of its breeding pipeline
IRRI is the primary developer and promoter of alternate wetting and drying as a water-saving and emission-reducing rice cultivation technique
AWD reduces methane emissions from flooded rice paddies while also saving water, making it a key climate mitigation practice in rice systems
Participatory variety selection is explicitly designed to identify varieties that meet farmer preferences, directly producing farmer-preferred varieties for adoption
Gender-responsive breeding is a specific application of gender transformative approaches within crop improvement, bridging Crop Improvement and Gender domains
AfricaRice combines indigenous germplasm with modern breeding tools including marker-assisted selection for rice improvement in Africa
Climate information services enable farmers to make better planting and management decisions, directly contributing to food security outcomes
Digital advisory platforms commonly integrate climate and weather information to provide actionable agro-advisories to farmers, bundling CIS with agricultural guidance
IWMI is a leading institution in developing and applying water accounting frameworks for agricultural water management globally
Community seed banks preserve local crop diversity and ensure farmer access to seeds during shocks, directly supporting food security and resilience
Agroforestry systems sequester carbon in tree biomass and soil, making them a key nature-based climate mitigation strategy bridging Farming Systems and Climate Mitigation domains
Silvopastoral systems can reduce enteric methane emissions per unit of livestock product through improved forage quality and reduced stocking density
ISFM practices that combine organic and inorganic inputs can reduce nitrous oxide emissions compared to sole mineral fertilizer use, bridging soil management and climate mitigation
IITA conducts biofortification research for cassava, yam, and other crops to address micronutrient deficiencies in Sub-Saharan Africa
The One Health approach explicitly integrates human, animal, and environmental health perspectives to address antimicrobial resistance as a cross-sectoral challenge
Zoonotic disease surveillance is a core component of One Health implementation, monitoring disease transmission at the human-animal-environment interface
Conservation agriculture practices including no-till and crop residue retention build soil organic carbon over time, contributing to climate mitigation
The tricot/triadic comparison approach is widely used in participatory plant breeding to decentralize variety evaluation with large numbers of farmer participants
CIP leads biofortification of sweetpotato with orange-fleshed varieties rich in beta-carotene, targeting vitamin A deficiency in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
Solar-powered irrigation replaces diesel pumps, reducing carbon emissions from agricultural water pumping while maintaining productivity
AI-driven agricultural extension platforms use machine learning algorithms to personalize recommendations and scale advisory services to large numbers of farmers
Agricultural and food security early warning systems rely heavily on satellite remote sensing for crop monitoring, drought detection, and yield forecasting
Restored rangelands sequester carbon in soil and vegetation biomass, making rangeland restoration a nature-based climate mitigation strategy
ILRI is a leading institution implementing One Health research integrating livestock, human, and environmental health dimensions
Nutrition-sensitive agriculture interventions are designed to improve dietary diversity as a key outcome measure, bridging food production and nutrition domains
Climate-smart village approach is inherently participatory, involving communities in testing and demonstrating climate adaptation technologies
Index-based agricultural insurance increasingly uses satellite data as objective indices to trigger payouts, replacing costly field assessments
Food systems transformation is recognized as essential for achieving climate mitigation goals, as food systems account for approximately one-third of global greenhouse gas emissions
Alliance of Bioversity and CIAT manages major gene bank collections and actively uses germplasm for crop improvement and conservation research
Modern digital soil mapping relies on machine learning algorithms to predict soil properties from remote sensing and environmental covariates
Crop simulation models are routinely coupled with climate projections to assess future impacts on agricultural productivity and guide adaptation planning
Community-based breeding programs for livestock improve productivity and income of smallholder farmers, directly contributing to household food security
Blended finance mechanisms are increasingly used to mobilize private investment for climate adaptation in agriculture, bridging finance and climate domains
Varieties developed through gender-responsive breeding that address women's specific trait preferences show higher adoption rates among women farmers
Participatory rangeland management creates governance structures and negotiated agreements that help reduce resource-based conflicts between herders and farmers
CGIAR breeding programs systematically use target product profiles to define market-oriented breeding objectives aligned with farmer and consumer needs
Biogas production from agricultural waste converts organic matter into energy and bioslurry fertilizer, exemplifying circular economy principles in farming systems
Scaling agricultural innovations to reach millions of farmers typically requires public-private partnerships to leverage private sector distribution networks and investment
Locally led climate adaptation explicitly integrates traditional ecological knowledge with scientific data to develop contextually appropriate adaptation strategies
Food environment assessments measure availability, accessibility, and affordability of diverse foods, directly informing interventions to improve dietary diversity
WorldFish is a leading research institution developing and promoting integrated rice-fish farming systems as a nutrition-sensitive and climate-smart approach
Anticipatory action is triggered by early warning signals from monitoring systems, requiring integration of forecast data with pre-agreed action protocols
Social protection programs including cash transfers directly improve household food security by increasing purchasing power and reducing vulnerability to shocks
Genome editing tools like CRISPR are being applied to develop disease-resistant crop varieties by precisely modifying susceptibility genes
Citizen science programs in agriculture rely on digital data collection tools including mobile apps to gather large-scale observations from farmer participants
Multifunctional landscape approaches integrate biodiversity conservation with agricultural production, explicitly targeting both food security and ecosystem health outcomes
Improving nitrogen use efficiency reduces nitrous oxide emissions from agricultural soils, a potent greenhouse gas, bridging soil management and climate mitigation
Demand-driven breeding programs rely on market intelligence to identify consumer and trader preferences that should guide variety development priorities

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Nutrition Security and Dietary Quality > Nutrition-Sensitive Approaches
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Food Systems, Nutrition, and Food Security > Nutrition Security and Dietary Quality
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Nutrition-sensitive agriculture is a cross-domain bridge concept that should be elevated as a parent category bridging Crop Improvement and Food Security rather than nested only under nutrition approaches

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Crop Improvement and Genetics > Plant Breeding Methods and Strategies > Specific Breeding Targets
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Food Systems, Nutrition, and Food Security > Nutrition Security and Dietary Quality > Nutrition-Sensitive Approaches
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Biofortification appears in both Crop Improvement and Food Security domains; its primary impact is nutritional, making it more appropriately a child of nutrition-sensitive approaches with a cross-reference to breeding

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Sustainable Farming Systems > Irrigation and Water Management > Irrigation Systems and Technologies
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Climate Change, Environment, and Natural Resources > Climate Mitigation and Low-Carbon Agriculture > Methane and Emission Reduction Practices
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: AWD is primarily recognized as a greenhouse gas mitigation practice for rice, not just a water-saving technology; it should be prominently placed in climate mitigation with a cross-reference to irrigation

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Other / Cross-cutting > One Health and Zoonotic Disease
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Sustainable Farming Systems > Livestock and Mixed Farming Systems > Herd Health Management
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: One Health approach is most directly applied in livestock health management contexts and should be anchored there rather than only in cross-cutting, while maintaining cross-domain links

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Digital Innovation and Data Science > Precision Agriculture and Smart Farming > Precision Farming Technologies
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Digital Innovation and Data Science > Decision Support and Modeling Tools > Decision Support Systems
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Digital twins function primarily as decision support and modeling tools for agricultural systems management, making them more conceptually aligned with decision support than precision farming hardware

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Food Systems, Nutrition, and Food Security > Food Systems Transformation > Food System Frameworks and Approaches
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Climate Change, Environment, and Natural Resources > Climate Adaptation and Resilience > Climate Resilience Building
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Food systems resilience is fundamentally a climate adaptation concept and should be co-located with climate resilience frameworks while maintaining links to food systems transformation

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Climate Change, Environment, and Natural Resources > Climate Adaptation and Resilience > Locally Led and Community-Based Adaptation
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Climate Change, Environment, and Natural Resources > Climate Information and Risk Management > Climate Information Services
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Participatory climate services is more specifically about co-designing climate information delivery than about locally led adaptation broadly; it fits better under climate information services

suggested_taxonomy_moves.from_branch: Sustainable Farming Systems > Farming Systems Innovation and Scaling > Technology Adoption and Diffusion
suggested_taxonomy_moves.to_branch: Capacity Development, Extension, and Knowledge Systems > Innovation Systems and Scaling > Scaling Strategies and Frameworks
suggested_taxonomy_moves.reason: Scaling readiness is a methodological framework for assessing innovation scalability and belongs with other scaling frameworks rather than under technology adoption dynamics
